channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Abandoned vehicle blocking bus stop on Trafalgar Road, 4th April
item.description:
Car abandoned in front of bus stop on busy Trafalgar Road for more than 2 days. SAFETY CONCERN FOR DRIVERS AND PEDESTRIANS

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Creed Passage, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken bin outside Central Chemist, 4th April
item.description:
Damaged, unusable bin with flytipping in surrounding area

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Waverley Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflow rubbish spread by foxes, 4th April
item.description:
Outside 18 pager rise the bin has been left with extra rubbish beside it and this had been spread by foxes. Is now across pavement and soiled.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Paget Rise, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: From 1105 HIGH RD TO 902 A1000, 4th April
item.description:
I am writing to formally report the severely deteriorated condition of the road surface at the specified location. Over the past four to five years, this stretch of road has been subject to repeated patchwork repairs. However, these temporary fixes have clearly failed, and the surface has now degraded to a level where individual pothole repairs are no longer effective or appropriate. The current condition presents serious safety risks to all road users: • Sudden changes in vehicle speed and direction to avoid potholes • Increased likelihood of loss of control, especially for motorcyclists and cyclists • Disruption to traffic flow, particularly on approach to junctions or higher-speed sections • Risk of vehicle damage, leading to unpredictable driver behaviour • Hazard escalation in wet or low-visibility conditions, where defects are harder to detect This is no longer a maintenance issue—it is a structural failure of the road surface. Given the extent of deterioration, I strongly request that this location is escalated beyond routine patch repairs and assessed for: Full carriageway resurfacing or reconstruction A long-term solution is now essential. Continued short-term patching is not only ineffective but also wastes public resources while leaving the safety risk unresolved. I would appreciate: 1. A clear action plan for this location 2. A defined timeframe for proper resurfacing works 3. Confirmation that this issue has been escalated to the appropriate highways team This matter requires urgent attention before it results in a serious incident. I look forward to your prompt response.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1-30, High Road, Whetstone, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large double bed mattress and bedstead dumped against wall at No 2., 4th April
item.description:

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Dupree Road, Charlton

item.title: 34b Bins on Pavement, 4th April
item.description:
Previously reported several months ago. 34b bins are constantly left on the pavement which causes obstructing and restriction on pavement. The property has a large driveway and owner does not keep bins on their property when not bin collection date. Instead keeps the bins on pavement. Thanks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Avenue Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
